page: Amazon Web Services gr6.4xlarge is a graphics-intensive, memory-optimized virtual machine powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 AMD EPYC 7R13 processor operating at 3.4 GHz, delivering 16 vCPUs across 8 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. The system is equipped with 128.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, establishing a 1:8 vCPU-to-RAM ratio. For hardware acceleration, it includes a single NVIDIA Ada Lovelace L4 GPU with 22 GB of dedicated VRAM, alongside 600 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and a 10 Gbps baseline network bandwidth.

Performance benchmarks indicate strong single-core capabilities, with stress-ng single-core CPU performance and Geekbench text processing scoring in the top 25%. Multi-core CPU performance, software compilation, and database operations fall within the average tier. Memory bandwidth tests show average read-only performance for both small and large blocks, though large-block write bandwidth is relatively weak, landing in the bottom 25%. The integrated NVIDIA L4 GPU delivers top-tier performance in the top 10% for LLM inference tasks, particularly in prompt processing and text generation for small and medium models.

From a resource density perspective, the gr6.4xlarge offers a balanced profile for workloads requiring both high memory capacity and GPU acceleration without the overhead of larger multi-GPU instances. The inclusion of local NVMe storage and a dedicated GPU makes it highly suitable for machine learning inference, small-to-medium LLM deployments, graphics-intensive rendering, and data science workflows that require substantial memory footprints alongside hardware acceleration.
description: Amazon Web Services gr6.4xlarge is a graphics-intensive, memory-optimized instance built on the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 AMD EPYC 7R13 processor with 16 vCPUs, 128.0 GB of DDR4 RAM, and a dedicated NVIDIA Ada Lovelace L4 GPU with 22 GB of VRAM. Storage is supported by a local 600 GB NVMe SSD, and network connectivity provides a 10 Gbps baseline. Benchmarks show strong single-core CPU performance and top-tier LLM inference speeds for small and medium models, though large-block memory write speeds are relatively weak. This hardware configuration is well-suited for machine learning inference, graphics rendering, and memory-intensive data processing tasks.
og_description: Amazon Web Services gr6.4xlarge is a graphics-intensive instance featuring 16 vCPUs, 128 GB of DDR4 RAM, a 600 GB NVMe SSD, and an NVIDIA Ada Lovelace L4 GPU with 22 GB of VRAM for accelerated workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services gr6.4xlarge features 16 vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, a 600 GB NVMe SSD, and an NVIDIA L4 GPU for memory-intensive and graphics workloads.
tagline: An accelerated virtual machine combining dedicated AMD EPYC processing, high-capacity DDR4 memory, and NVIDIA Ada Lovelace GPU acceleration.
bullet_points: Powered by an AMD EPYC 7R13 processor with 16 vCPUs and 128 GB DDR4 RAM
Equipped with a dedicated NVIDIA Ada Lovelace L4 GPU featuring 22 GB VRAM
Includes 600 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and 10 Gbps baseline network bandwidth
Delivers top-tier benchmark performance for small and medium LLM inference
Best suited for machine learning inference, graphics rendering, and data science
